title: Building an HPC BLAST Pipeline
author: Bonnie Hurwitz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d5a82d
materials: Gordon Betty Moore Foundation Marine Microbes peptide database (CAM_PROJ_MarineMicrobes.pep.fa)

steps.title: Login to HPC
steps.instruction: Login to the HPC (at the University of Arizona) with your user name. Note that this script runs on the head node, is not compute intensive, and creates smaller files before distributing data for BLAST jobs. Use `ssh username@login.hpc.arizona.edu`.

steps.title: Create Project Directory
steps.instruction: Create a directory for the pipeline and navigate into it: `mkdir hpc-blast` then `cd hpc-blast`.

steps.title: Create Subdirectories
steps.instruction: Inside the `hpc-blast` directory, create subdirectories to hold standard error, standard output, data, BLAST database, BLAST results, and scripts: `mkdir std-err std-out data blastdb blast-results scripts`.

steps.title: Create BLAST Database
steps.instruction: Go to the `blastdb` directory. Load iRODS and BLAST modules. Get the peptides from the Gordon Betty Moore Foundation Marine Microbes in iPlant. Create the BLAST database to compare against. Then go up a directory. Example commands: `cd blastdb` `module load irods` `iget /iplant/home/shared/imicrobe/projects/43/CAM_PROJ_MarineMicrobes.pep.fa` `module load blast` `makeblastdb -in CAM_PROJ_MarineMicrobes.pep.fa -dbtype prot` `cd ..`.

steps.title: Copy Input FASTA Files
steps.instruction: Copy the input files (day/night transcriptomes) from iPlant. Go to the `data` directory, change directories in iRODS to the day/night transcriptome data directory, then get the day and night transcriptome FASTA files. Finally, go up a directory. Example commands: `cd data` `icd /iplant/home/shared/imicrobe/class/night_day` `iget SRR006596_day.fasta` `iget SRR006597_night.fasta` `cd ..`.

steps.title: Copy Perl Scripts
steps.instruction: Copy two Perl scripts (`fasta-splitter.pl` and `bio-searchio.pl`) from your homework into the `scripts` directory. Note: you will need to change the path to point to the location of the scripts in your home directory. Example commands: `cd scripts` `cp /my-path/week10/01-fasta-splitter.pl 01-fasta-splitter.pl` `cp /my-path/week11/01-bio-searchio.pl 01-bio-searchio.pl` `cd ..`.

steps.title: Create config.sh Script
steps.instruction: Create the `config.sh` script to define environmental variables and BLAST parameters for user modification. Use `vi config.sh` and enter the following text: #!/bin/bash # directories we need export CWD=$PWD export SCRIPT_DIR="$CWD/scripts" export FASTA_DIR="$CWD/data" export STDERR_DIR="$CWD/std-err" export STDOUT_DIR="$CWD/std-out" export SPLIT_FA_DIR="$FASTA_DIR/faSplit" export SPLIT_READ_CT="25000" export BLAST_OUT_DIR="$CWD/blast-results" # BLAST parameters export EVAL="1e-10" export BLAST="blastx" export BLASTDB="$CWD/blastdb/CAM_PROJ_MarineMicrobes.pep.fa"

steps.title: get the env variables and create...
steps.instruction: get the env variables and create a list of files source ./config.sh #

steps.title: get all of the fasta files...
steps.instruction: get all of the fasta files for input cd "$FASTA_DIR" export FILES_LIST="$FASTA_DIR/files-list" pwd ls *.fasta | sed "s/^\.\///" > $FILES_LIST #

steps.title: split the fasta files into smaller chunks $SCRIPT_DIR/run-fasta-splitter
steps.instruction: split the fasta files into smaller chunks $SCRIPT_DIR/run-fasta-splitter.sh #

steps.title: run blast on each of the...
steps.instruction: run blast on each of the file chunks while read FILE; do export FILE="$FILE" cd $SPLIT_FA_DIR export SPLIT_FILES_LIST="$SPLIT_FA_DIR/split.$FILE" ls $FILE.* > $SPLIT_FILES_LIST NUM_SPLIT_FILES=$(wc -l $SPLIT_FILES_LIST | cut -d ' ' -f 1) ## run blast on each of the smaller chunks against a blast database JOB_ID1=$(qsub -v SCRIPT_DIR,SPLIT_FA_DIR,BLAST,EVAL,BLASTDB,BLAST_OUT_DIR,FILE -N blast -e "$STDERR_DIR" -o "$STDOUT_DIR" -J 1-$NUM_SPLIT_FILES $SCRIPT_DIR/run-blast.sh) ## parse the blast results for each of the chunked files JOB_ID2=$(qsub -v SCRIPT_DIR,BLAST_OUT_DIR,FILE -W depend=afterany:$JOB_ID1 -e "$STDERR_DIR" -o "$STDOUT_DIR" -J 1-$NUM_SPLIT_FILES $SCRIPT_DIR/run-parse-blast.sh) done < $FILES_LIST

steps.title: Create run-fasta-splitter.sh Script
steps.instruction: Create the `run-fasta-splitter.sh` shell script to run the Perl fasta splitter. This script runs on the head node. Navigate to the `scripts` directory, use `vi run-fasta-splitter.sh` and enter the following text: #!/bin/bash source /usr/share/Modules/init/bash if [[ ! -d "$SPLIT_FA_DIR" ]]; then echo Cannot find faSplit "$SPLIT_FA_DIR" mkdir -p $SPLIT_FA_DIR fi if [[ ! -e "$FILES_LIST" ]]; then echo Cannot find files list "$FILES_LIST" exit 1 fi cd $FASTA_DIR while read FILE; do echo $FILE $SCRIPT_DIR/01-fasta-splitter.pl -n $SPLIT_READ_CT -o $SPLIT_FA_DIR $FILE done < $FILES_LIST

steps.title: Create run-blast.sh Script
steps.instruction: Create the `run-blast.sh` shell script to execute BLAST on a compute node. Configure PBS directives for resource allocation (e.g., walltime=24:00:00). Use `vi run-blast.sh` and enter the following text: #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bhurwitz #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=2:mem=4gb #PBS -l place=pack:shared #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -M youruser@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea # BLAST parameters NUM_THREADS=2 # make sure this is requested in the above "select" DESC=10 ALN=10 MAX_HSPS=10 module load blast cd "$SPLIT_FA_DIR" FASTA="$SPLIT_FA_DIR/$FILE.${PBS_ARRAY_INDEX}" # run blast on each file chunk $BLAST -query $FASTA -db $BLASTDB -evalue $EVAL -out $BLAST_OUT_DIR/$FILE.${PBS_ARRAY_INDEX}.blastout -max_hsps $MAX_HSPS -num_descriptions $DESC -num_alignments $ALN -num_threads $NUM_THREADS

steps.title: Create run-parse-blast.sh Script
steps.instruction: Create the `run-parse-blast.sh` shell script to parse BLAST output on a compute node. Include PBS directives and load necessary modules (blast, perl). Use `vi run-parse-blast.sh` and enter the following text: #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bhurwitz #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=2:mem=4gb #PBS -l place=pack:shared #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -M youruser@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea module load blast cd $BLAST_OUT_DIR BLOUT="$BLAST_OUT_DIR/$FILE.${PBS_ARRAY_INDEX}.blastout" echo File "$FILE.${PBS_ARRAY_INDEX}.blastout" $SCRIPT_DIR/04-blast-search.pl $BLOUT > $BLOUT.parsed

steps.title: Modify blast-search Perl Script
steps.instruction: Modify the `04-blast-search.pl` (or `01-bio-searchio.pl`) Perl script. Update the e-value to `my $min = 1e-10;` and modify the print statement to include additional hit information: `say join "\t", $result->query_name, $hit->name, $hit->description, $hsp->percent_identity, $hsp->length('total'), $hsp->evalue;`

steps.title: Make Scripts Executable & Run Pipeline
steps.instruction: Make all `.sh` and `.pl` scripts executable using `chmod 755 *sh *pl` and then run the pipeline using `./blast-pipeline.sh`. Make sure your scripts are executable before running. If there are no errors, the pipeline should complete in less than one day.

steps.title: Monitor Pipeline Status
steps.instruction: Check the pipeline's running status using `qstat -u yourusername` (e.g., `qstat -u bhurwitz`).

steps.title: Check Standard Error & Output
steps.instruction: Once the pipeline finishes (no jobs listed under `qstat`), check the `std-err` and `std-out` directories. In `std-err`, files should be zero length. In `std-out`, examine job descriptions and compute time. You can also perform these checks while the pipeline is running.

steps.title: Commit Code to GitHub
steps.instruction: Commit your code to your own GitHub repository using the directory: `hpc-blast`.
